attacking_style: Explosive attacks inside final 1km on steep gradients (20%+) or mountain TT dominance. Prefers decisive late moves rather than long-range efforts, frequently sprinting for bonus seconds even when leading GC.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma deploys devastating high-tempo leadouts via KUSS/domestiques to isolate rivals before Roglič's final acceleration. Team willing to sacrifice stage wins for GC time gains. Occasionally exposed when isolated early on climbs.
terrain_preference: Excels on short, steep explosive gradients (20%+ walls) and mountain time trials. Vulnerable on long, steady HC climbs and flat TTs against pure power specialists. Technical descents show mixed results—capable but crash-prone.
vulnerability: Catastrophic time trial collapses under extreme pressure (2020 Tour Stage 20). Exposed on long sustained climbs when isolated without domestiques. Crash-prone in technical finales and descents. Cold/wet conditions caused tactical failures (clothing issues).
key_patterns: Hunts bonus seconds aggressively even with comfortable GC leads, showing killer instinct over conservation.
Attacks inside final 500m on summit finishes repeatedly, superior anaerobic reserves after sustained climbs.
Wins mountain TTs through superior pacing discipline, negative-splitting to crush rivals in final kilometers.
Crashes at critical moments across multiple Grand Tours, durability concern despite strong recovery ability.
Dominates Vuelta specifically: 3 consecutive titles (2019-2021), 10+ stage wins, comfortable in third-week racing.
Team tension visible when stronger than designated leader (2023 Vuelta with Kuss/Vingegaard).
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Giro win via mountain TT recovery demonstrates elite form despite mid-race crashes. Catalunya/Tirreno dominance shows successful transition post-Jumbo conflicts. Resilient after 2020 Tour collapse.
